falsification_conditions.condition: S&P 500 closes below 4000 (key support level) for 3 consecutive trading days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Confirms a breakdown in the pattern, invalidating the 'HOLD' signal and suggesting a deeper bearish trend rather than a bottom.

falsification_conditions.condition: U.S. Non-Farm Payrolls report shows employment growth of +250K or more in the next 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Strong labor market data would contradict the 'bottom' pattern assumptions, proving the current call wrong by indicating sustained economic strength.

falsification_conditions.condition: VIX (Volatility Index) spikes above 30 and remains above 30 for 5 consecutive trading days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: Sustained extreme volatility would invalidate the 'HOLD' signal, indicating a blowoff phase or panic selling rather than a stable bottom formation.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
